name: Ginger-Banana Chutney
recipeIngredient: 6 Ripe bananas, put through a food mill
2 cups Fresh or unsweetened canned pineapple juice
recipeInstructions:
Most people think of Trinidad, Guyana, and Martinique when they think of East Indian influences on Caribbean cooking. Jamaica, however, has had its share of that influence as well, as proved by this chutney prepared from bananas and ginger. Makes about 3 1/2 cups.
Place the onions, bananas, dates, and vinegar in a nonreactive saucepan. Stir to mix well and cook over low heat for 20 minutes. Add the remaining ingredients and stir until well combined. Continue cooking until the chutney has a jam-like consistency. Remove from heat and place in sterilized jars. This chutney can accompany cold meats, grilled meats, and of course, curries. It will keep for 3 weeks in the refrigerator.
